notes: Provider resolution precondition is the same as vk_call_debug_success_v1; the failure trigger comes from the replay placeholder peer target, which materializes to a plain UDP socket instead of a DTLS peer.
The legacy runtime path fails the session with exit_code=1 when DTLS handshake cannot complete against that peer, but it does not expose a structured stage name for the error.
The rewrite failure path is replayed deterministically by TestRuntimeEvidenceReplay using provider=vk, fixture-driven staged HTTP resolution, a valid TURN allocation, and a plain UDP peer.
cmd/tunnel-client/main.go maps stage-wrapped runtime failures to exit code 1 and prints stage=dtls_handshake.

deviations: Rewrite emits the structured stage dtls_handshake when the replay peer does not speak DTLS; the legacy client exposes only an untyped runtime failure for the same supported slice.
